mapping.a_element: wallets that co-fire as early buyers

mapping.b_element: Deffuant confidence threshold controlling cluster count

brief_justification: Paper A is an empirical detection-plus-causal-inference study: union-find on co-occurrence graphs surfaces wallet cohorts, then random-matched, activity-matched-placebo and propensity-score designs test for a causal buyer-flow effect and reveal selection bias. Paper B is a coupled nonlinear multi-agent dynamical model (attraction-repulsion motion plus Deffuant bounded-confidence opinion dynamics) whose confidence threshold produces emergent opinion clusters. The overlap is only vocabulary ('coordinated agents / clusters / thresholds'); the governing machinery — static graph clustering with statistical causal inference versus an evolving dynamical law generating clusters — does not correspond role-for-role.
